payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

original_descriptions: Enable children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks
Provide children visibility into task inventory
Enable children to view their weekly earnings summary
Enable parents to access child performance reporting for compensation distribution
Weekly aggregation of earned amounts for child presentation
Parent reporting of aggregated child earnings and task completion data

payload.evaluated_scope: All input material in this project

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Task claiming and tracking
payload.justification: Signal describes claiming and tracking as strategic-level functions the enterprise performs

payload.description: Task assignment definition
payload.justification: Signal describes defining compensated task assignments as a strategic process capability

payload.description: Performance reporting
payload.justification: Signal identifies accessing and generating child performance reports as strategic function

payload.description: Task completion record for pocket money
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference tasks as strategic things that are defined, assigned, claimed, tracked, and have completion status | Signals reference completed tasks, task inventory status, and historical task records as distinct tracked things

payload.description: Monetary compensation value
payload.justification: Signals identify monetary values and earnings as strategically important things associated with tasks

payload.description: Child performance report
payload.justification: Signal explicitly identifies reporting on child performance as a strategic information thing

payload.description: Weekly compensation cycle
payload.justification: Signals reference weekly context for task tracking and earnings summary, indicating strategic temporal cycle

payload.description: Child user
payload.justification: Multiple signals identify children as distinct strategic stakeholders who claim tasks, view inventory, and access earnings

payload.description: Parent user
payload.justification: Signals explicitly identify parents as distinct strategic stakeholders who define tasks and access performance reports

payload.description: Enable children to view and claim available pocket money tasks
payload.justification: Signal directly expresses fundamental purpose at strategic level for child task claiming and tracking capability | Signal captures strategic purpose of transparency for children regarding available and completed tasks

payload.description: Enable parents to define compensated task assignments with monetary values
payload.justification: Signal articulates parental motivation as strategic goal for task definition and compensation capability

payload.description: Enable tracking and reporting of completed tasks and earned compensation
payload.justification: Signal identifies motivational goal for child access to earnings information | Signal establishes parental need as strategic goal for performance visibility supporting payment accuracy

payload.description: Provide longitudinal task history access for accountability and trend analysis
payload.justification: Signal indicates stakeholder requirement for historical data access supporting strategic accountability objectives

payload.description: Child claiming completed tasks for monetary reward
payload.justification: Signal establishes the core business process of children claiming tasks to track earnings.

payload.description: Parent specification of tasks with monetary values
payload.justification: Signal defines the parent capability to create and configure task entities with associated rewards.

payload.description: Child viewing of available and completed tasks
payload.justification: Signal defines child visibility capability for task entities within temporal scope.

payload.description: Weekly aggregation of child earnings
payload.justification: Signal establishes the business function of calculating and displaying weekly monetary totals.

payload.description: Parent reporting of child-task-earnings relationships
payload.justification: Signal defines parent reporting capability to support payment fulfillment business function.

payload.description: Task availability resets at weekly boundaries
payload.justification: Signal specifies the cyclical business rule governing task availability lifecycle.

payload.description: Historical task data must be retained and accessible
payload.justification: Signal mandates the business rule for historical data retention across periods.

payload.description: Task entity with multiple states (available, completed, with monetary value)
payload.justification: Signal explicitly defines task entities with monetary value attributes as core business data objects. | Signals distinguish completed tasks as a separate entity state relevant to claiming and earning processes. | Signals identify available tasks as distinct entities with visibility requirements and cyclical reset behavior.

payload.description: Child as participant in task system
payload.justification: Signals identify child as a distinct business entity linked to tasks and having visibility requirements.

payload.description: Weekly earnings aggregate
payload.justification: Signal defines weekly aggregated monetary totals as a distinct business data object visible to children.

payload.description: Historical task data
payload.justification: Signal mandates retention of historical task entities across temporal periods as accessible data objects.

payload.description: Monetary value of task
payload.justification: Signal explicitly identifies monetary value as an attribute associated with task entities.

payload.description: Child claims completed task
payload.justification: Signals define the linkage relationship between child entities and completed task entities through claiming action.

payload.description: Child-task-earnings association for payment tracking
payload.justification: Signal establishes the relationship between child, task, and earnings entities for parent reporting purposes.

payload.description: Weekly cycle for task availability and earnings aggregation
payload.justification: Signal explicitly defines weekly temporal boundaries as cyclical business constraint for task reset. | Signal establishes weekly temporal cycle for earnings calculation and reporting.

payload.description: Task completion event
payload.justification: Signals reference completed tasks as a significant business state change event within the system.

payload.description: Parent role
payload.justification: Signals identify parent as a distinct business role with task specification and reporting capabilities.

payload.description: Child role
payload.justification: Signals identify child as a distinct business actor with task claiming, viewing, and earnings tracking responsibilities.

payload.description: Track monetary rewards for completed tasks
payload.justification: Signals indicate the core business objective of tracking earnings through task completion.

payload.description: Support parent payment fulfillment
payload.justification: Signal explicitly states the business objective of parent reporting to enable payment processes.

payload.description: Child visibility limited to temporal scope
payload.justification: Signal defines business constraint on child actor visibility requirements within temporal boundaries.

payload.description: Historical data must remain accessible across periods
payload.justification: Signal mandates business constraint for retention and accessibility of historical task information.

payload.description: Child users may access task collections according to visibility requirements
payload.justification: Data visibility requirements specify logical access patterns, which constitute a business rule governing how child users interact with task data.

payload.description: Weekly aggregation of child earnings and task completion data for parent reporting
payload.justification: Temporal aggregation logic describes a logical function that computes and presents weekly earnings to children. | Logical reporting interface represents a function providing parents with aggregated analytical views.

payload.description: Task completion event links to child entity
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes a logical association rule linking task completion events to child entities, which is a relationship at the Designer level.

payload.description: Weekly task availability restoration cycle
payload.justification: Periodic state reset logic defining weekly cycle behavior explicitly describes a recurring temporal cycle at the logical level.

payload.description: Historical task records must be logically persistent and accessible
payload.justification: Data retention requirement mandating persistence and accessibility represents a design constraint on the logical data architecture.

payload.description: Operating system version compatibility constraint for platform deployment
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes a platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete operating system versions for deployment, which is a technical constraint at the Builder level governing deployment decisions.

payload.description: Weekly earned amount total access event for child users
payload.justification: SG542 establishes weekly operational schedule boundary for earned amount access; represents detailed timing specification for when this access event occurs.

payload.description: Weekly task availability reset cycle timing specification
payload.justification: SG544 specifies weekly operational cycle timing for task reset; detailed sequencing specification for recurring weekly cycle operations.

payload.description: iOS mobile platform deployment node
payload.justification: SG545 identifies iOS as one of three distinct named deployment platforms; Rule 5 requires separate CCI per named platform instance. | SG545 identifies Android as one of three distinct named deployment platforms; Rule 5 requires separate CCI per named platform instance.

payload.description: Windows desktop platform deployment node
payload.justification: SG545 identifies Windows as one of three distinct named deployment platforms; Rule 5 requires separate CCI per named platform instance.

payload.description: Child user interface for task claiming operations
payload.justification: SG539 identifies children as operational actors executing task claiming activities; at Row 5 this translates to the detailed user interface specification for child interactions.

payload.description: Parent user interface for task configuration and monetary assignment
payload.justification: SG540 identifies parents as operational actors responsible for configuration and value assignment; Row 5 requires detailed interface specification for parent activities.

payload.description: Child user interface for viewing available and completed tasks within weekly cycles
payload.justification: SG541 defines operational visibility requirements for child actors; represents detailed interface specification for task viewing capabilities.

payload.description: Parent user interface for accessing pocket money distribution reports
payload.justification: SG543 defines parent operational role in report access and distribution activities; detailed interface specification for parent reporting functions.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the fundamental purpose of enabling children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks weekly.
Articulates parental motivation to define compensated task assignments with associated monetary values.
Captures the purpose of providing children visibility into available and completed task inventory.
Identifies the motivational goal of enabling children to view their weekly earnings summary.
Establishes parental need to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution purposes.
Indicates stakeholder requirement for longitudinal task history access supporting accountability or trend analysis.
Section header indicating the commencement of conceptual system description.
Establishes the core business concept of children claiming completed tasks for monetary reward tracking.
Defines the parent capability to specify task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Specifies the relationship between completed task entities and child entities through linkage.
Defines child actor visibility requirements for both available and completed task entities within temporal scope.
Establishes weekly aggregation capability for earned monetary totals visible to child actors.
Defines parent reporting capability displaying child-task-earnings relationships to support payment fulfillment.
Specifies the cyclical business rule for task availability reset at weekly temporal boundaries.
Mandates retention and accessibility of historical task data entities across temporal periods.
Logical association rule defining how task completion events are linked to child entities.
Data visibility requirements specifying logical access patterns for child users to task collections.
Temporal aggregation logic defining weekly computation and presentation of earned amounts to children.
Logical reporting interface providing parents aggregated view of child earnings and task completion data.
Periodic state reset logic defining weekly cycle behavior for task availability restoration.
Data retention requirement mandating logical persistence and accessibility of historical task records.
Platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete operating system versions for deployment.
Identifies children as operational actors who execute task claiming activities throughout the week on a temporal basis.
Identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task configuration and monetary value assignment activities.
Defines operational visibility requirements for child actors viewing available and completed tasks within weekly cycles.
Establishes weekly operational schedule boundary when child actors can access earned amount totals.
Defines parent operational role in accessing reports to execute pocket money distribution activities.
Specifies weekly operational cycle timing when task availability resets for claiming operations.
Defines deployment platform locations specifying where the system operates across iOS, Android, and Windows environments.
